Table 4: Performance Comparison of SSMamba with 11 SOTA Methods on 10 ROI Pathology Datasets. Bold: the best result. Underline: the second best result.

Figure 1: The Architecture of the SSMamba. (a) Overall architecture of SSMamba. (b) The original Mamba Module. (c) The proposed DMS Module. (d) The proposed LPR Module. (e) The SSMamba decoder for MAMIM.

Figure 4: Grad-CAM visualization of the influence of different modules on the feature representation of ROI pathology images. ((i) original image; (ii) SSMamba with Linear projection; (iii) SSMamba with Patch Merge; (iv) SSMamba with Local Perception Unit (LPU); (v) SSMamba with LPR (our final model); (vi) SSMamba using traditional Mamba modules; (vii) SSMamba without pre-training; (viii) SSMamba pre-trained in CL.)
Figure 5: Masked Reconstruction Samples on ROI Image (masking ratio: 75%). Left: Masked input; Middle: SSMamba reconstruction; Right: Ground truth.
Table 5: Performance Evaluation of Cross-Dataset Generalization.(The training data set is NCT. The test data sets correspond to MHIST and CRC, respectively.)
Figure 2: Bubble plot of the effectiveness of SSMamba.
Table 11: Performance Evaluation of the DMS Module on Remaining Datasets ((LaC, PAIP2019, SIPa, and Ost). (“o/DMS” indicates without the DMS module, while “w/DMS” indicates with the DMS module.)
Table 12: Performance Evaluation of the LPR Module on Remaining Datasets (LaC, PAIP2019, SIPa, and Ost).
